steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage states "not all cascades are beneficial," then the blank introduces a study about warblers. The study is not a new claim or a contrast — it is concrete proof that supports what was just stated. The blank needs a transition that signals confirming evidence follows.

steps.title: Test each choice against the relationship
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Instead,': signals a substitution or alternative, but no replacement is being offered — the study proves the claim, not replaces it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In response,': signals a reaction to something, but the study is not a reaction to the claim — it is evidence of it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Indeed,': signals that confirming evidence follows, which fits perfectly — the warbler example proves that cascades can be harmful. ✓
  • Choice D — 'In addition,': signals a new parallel point, but the study does not add a new claim — it supports the one just made. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
